one_time_description: First Nations Health Authority Capital grant of $2,500,000 (non-recurring); also fuel leak remediation costs of $3,581,624 recorded in Nation-owned enterprises
economic_notes: Nation owns Red Bluff Development Corporation Ltd., RBDC Trucking LLP, 1305049 BC Ltd., Sus Yaz Early Learning and After School Center Ltd., and Lhtako Gas and Convenience Store Ltd. Underground fuel leak discovered at Lhtako Gas and Convenience Store from December 2020 installation; estimated 105,000 litres of premium gasoline leaked. ISC contributed $2,379,425 toward remediation. Litigation commenced against installation contractor. Nation has investments in Castle Mountain Hydro (2.5% interest) and Dene Developments. West Fraser Timber Mills and mining companies (BGM/Osisko, Spanish Mountain Gold) provide revenue. Rita Road Marriott Hotel under construction.
notes: 5 GBEs accounted for by modified equity method (Red Bluff Development Corporation, RBDC Trucking LLP, 1305049 BC Ltd., Sus Yaz Early Learning and After School Center Ltd., Lhtako Gas and Convenience Store Ltd.). Nation-owned enterprises showed a loss of $821,425 due largely to $3,581,624 fuel leak remediation costs at Lhtako Gas and Convenience, partially offset by $2,379,425 ISC contribution. Large FNFA loan proceeds of $17,537,545 drew long-term debt from $8,363,817 to $25,655,737 — likely related to Rita Road Marriott Hotel construction and DL 8007 land acquisition. BC Housing Commission interim financing of $4,645,086 also notable. No schedule of remuneration included. ISC funding reconciliation shows $4,306,186 on ISC confirmation but only $1,932,186 on statement of operations due to $2,374,000 allocated to Lhtako Gas and Convenience. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $1,932,186 + FNHA $354,652 + FNHA Capital $2,500,000 + Gov Canada $734,465 + CMHC $277002 + Province of BC $4,314,275 + BC Aboriginal Child Care Society $298,549 + New Relationship Trust $240,000 + Carrier Chilcotin Tribal Council $75,375 + CCATEC $53,811 + FNESS $131,804 = $11,912,119; OSR = 14,370,030 - 11,912,119 = 2,457,911. Note: revenue_lines total $14,370,030 per statement; Canada Class Action Water Settlement was $0 in 2024 (vs $500,000 in 2023).
